name: Set Effect
content:
This block applies effects to all sounds played in a function. Currently there are three effects: - Volume: Changes the loudness of the function - Filter: Changes the tone of the function, making the sounds muted or muffled. - Delay: Adds an echo to the sounds
Effects are a way for record producers to change how a track sounds
You can use them with different tracks to change how a song sounds or feels. In this example, the backing tracks will be at a lower volume than the main chorus
Sequencing is important with effects! Once you set an effect, it applies to every block that comes after it unless you change it
You can also set multiple effects and they will all apply together
There are three types of effects available in Music Lab: volume, filter, and delay.
Key Takeaway: Effect blocks change how a song sounds and feels. They affect all of the blocks that come after them.

palette_params.name: effect
palette_params.description:
The effect to apply to the function.

- Volume: Changes the loudness of the function
- Filter: Changes the tone of the function, making the sounds muted or muffled.
- Delay: Adds an echo to the sounds

palette_params.name: amount
palette_params.description:
The amount of the effect. The options are:

Volume: 
 * Full: Plays sounds at their normal volume
 * Medium: Plays sounds at a lower volume
 * Low: Plays sounds quietly

Delay:
* High: Adds a large amount of delay
* Low: Adds a small amount of delay
* Off: Turns off the delay effect

Filter: 
* Medium: Reduces tinny sounds like cymbals and noise, making the sounds seem muffled
* Low: Reduces most sounds except low parts like bass drums & bass guitar, making the sounds seem very muffled
* Off: Turns off the filter effect

short_description: Add an effect, like delay (e.g. an ecgo) or volume (loudness) of sounds.
tips: You can change the amounts of effects to make your track more interesting over time. For instance, it's common to use the 'filter' effect to have a beat or bass line grow or build before a big change in a song.
